name: awesome-android-root
description: Discover best root apps, Magisk/ KernelSu & LSPosed(xposed) modules & rooting guides
readme_summary: Awesome Android Root is a comprehensive resource hub for Android rooting enthusiasts. It curates over 500 top root apps, Magisk, KernelSU, and LSPosed modules, along with detailed rooting guides for various devices. The repository aims to be the ultimate destination for discovering and managing tools and information related to rooting Android devices.
key_features: Curated list of 500+ root apps and modules
Support for Magisk, KernelSU, and LSPosed (Xposed)
Step-by-step rooting guides for various devices
Categorized organization of apps and modules (Privacy, AI, App Control, Performance, Gaming, etc.)
Glossary of rooting terms
Starter Kit for essential apps
Web app for enhanced navigation and search
primary_use_case: To serve as a centralized, curated, and easily navigable resource for discovering and learning about Android rooting tools, modules, and guides.
cover_image_prompt: A wise old librarian in a vast, magical library, surrounded by glowing Android devices. The librarian is carefully placing ancient-looking scrolls, each labeled with app or module names like 'Magisk', 'KernelSU', and 'LSPosed', onto shelves organized by categories such as 'Privacy', 'Performance', and 'Gaming'. Digital particles representing data flow from the scrolls to a central holographic display showing a simplified Android device being 'unlocked' or 'enhanced'. The repository name 'Awesome Android Root' is subtly etched into a wooden signpost. The scene is rendered in a warm, inviting digital painting style with clear outlines and a focus on the magical, organized nature of knowledge.

name: Reversecore_MCP
description: A security-first MCP server that empowers AI agents to perform automated reverse engineering, malware analysis, forensics, vulnerability research, and SAST — powered by Radare2, YARA, LIEF, Capstone, and more.
readme_summary: Reversecore MCP is an enterprise-grade server that integrates over 50 analysis tools to enable AI agents to perform advanced security tasks like reverse engineering, malware analysis, and vulnerability research through natural language commands. It acts as a bridge, translating human intent into complex, multi-tool security analysis workflows.
key_features: AI-Powered Security Analysis
Integrated Static & Dynamic Analysis
50+ Analysis Tools (Radare2, YARA, LIEF, Capstone, etc.)
Natural Language Interface for Complex Tasks
Automated Workflow Execution
MITRE ATT&CK Mapping
SAST for Python, C, C++
Digital Forensics Capabilities
Enterprise-grade MCP Server
primary_use_case: To empower AI agents and security professionals to conduct sophisticated reverse engineering, malware analysis, vulnerability research, and digital forensics by translating natural language queries into automated, multi-tool analysis workflows.
cover_image_prompt: A wise, ancient librarian wearing spectacles, standing in a vast, organized library filled with glowing books and scrolls. The librarian is using a magical quill to write on a large, ethereal parchment, transforming complex, tangled lines of code and binary data (represented as glowing particles) into clear, structured reports and diagrams. The parchment displays a visual metaphor of a complex lock being easily opened. Subtle holographic interfaces showing code snippets and analysis results float around the librarian. The overall mood is one of intelligence and effortless mastery over complexity. The style should be a detailed digital painting with a focus on glowing elements and a rich, deep color palette.

name: skills
description: Agent Skills for Google products and technologies
readme_summary: This repository provides a collection of pre-built 'Agent Skills' designed to integrate with Google products and technologies, particularly Google Cloud services. These skills enable developers to easily add complex functionalities to their AI agents, streamlining development and interaction with Google's ecosystem.
key_features: Pre-built Agent Skills for Google products and technologies
Integration with Google Cloud services (e.g., Gemini API, BigQuery, GKE, Firebase)
Modular installation via `npx skills add`
Skills cover a wide range of Google Cloud functionalities, from basics to advanced topics like AI/ML and Well-Architected Frameworks
Includes skills for Google Advertising and Measurement APIs
primary_use_case: To provide developers with ready-to-use components (skills) that enhance AI agents, allowing them to interact with and leverage Google Cloud services and other Google technologies more efficiently.
cover_image_prompt: A skilled artisan in a workshop, meticulously assembling intricate clockwork mechanisms representing various Google Cloud services like BigQuery, GKE, and Firebase. Each component is labeled with a subtle Google Cloud logo. The artisan uses glowing digital blueprints that materialize into functional parts. In the background, a large, stylized 'Agent Platform' acts as a workbench, where the assembled mechanisms are being integrated into a central, intelligent agent. The overall mood is one of creation and seamless integration, with a clean, modern aesthetic and a palette of Google's brand colors. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with soft lighting and clear, crisp details.

name: whichllm
description: Find the local LLM that actually runs and performs best on your hardware. Ranked by real, recency-aware benchmarks, not parameter count. One command, run it instantly.
readme_summary: whichllm is a command-line tool that helps users find and run the best-performing Large Language Models (LLMs) locally on their specific hardware. It benchmarks models based on real-world performance and hardware compatibility, rather than just parameter count, providing a ranked list of suitable LLMs.
key_features: Auto-detects hardware (GPU/CPU/RAM)
Ranks LLMs from HuggingFace based on real benchmarks and hardware fit
Supports various installation methods (uvx, pip, brew)
Allows simulation of different hardware configurations
Offers options for safer or more aggressive model recommendations
Provides common workflows for different use cases (e.g., comparing upgrades, planning GPU needs, running models)
Outputs results in human-readable, markdown, or JSON formats
Can generate Python snippets for running selected models
primary_use_case: To help users identify and select the most performant local LLM that can run effectively on their specific computer hardware, bypassing simple parameter count metrics.
cover_image_prompt: A wise, old librarian meticulously organizing a vast library of glowing, ethereal books, each representing a different Large Language Model. The librarian is using a special, intricate device that analyzes the 'weight' and 'energy' of each book, sorting them onto shelves that perfectly match the dimensions of a nearby, stylized computer workstation. Some books are placed on the workstation's 'GPU' shelf, others on its 'RAM' shelf, with a clear indicator showing which books fit perfectly and which are too large or too small. The 'whichllm' logo is subtly integrated into the librarian's spectacles. The scene is rendered in a warm, inviting digital painting style with a focus on clarity and a touch of magic.
